s: The grammar pattern にひきかえ means 'in sharp contrast with; compared to'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'にひきかえ' mean?
a: in sharp contrast with; compared to
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: The son, in stark contrast to his meticulous father, was slovenly.

s: The grammar pattern でなくては means 'if not.. then noone'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'でなくては' mean?
e: noone but him can do this

s: The grammar pattern ばかりに means 'because (of a negative thing); simply because; simply on account of'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ばかりに' mean?
a: because (of a negative thing); simply because; simply on account of
d: with 〜, some continuing activity will end (or a prohibition on an activity will start)
Brings about an effect on; relates to; a matter of
e: he was fired just for talking back

s: The grammar pattern 果たして means 'really, as was expected'
q: What does the grammar pattern '果たして' mean?
e: is it really true?

s: The grammar pattern どんな means 'Whatever; whoever, etc.'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'どんな' mean?
e: Whatever happened

s: The grammar pattern 今ごろになって ~も means 'it's already too late'
q: What does the grammar pattern '今ごろになって ~も' mean?
d: would be much better, would be acceptable
e: It's too late to say you're sorry!

s: The grammar pattern あっての means 'Thanks to (various things), (outcome)'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'あっての' mean?
d: consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
to repeat the same action over
to attempt something again and again
after a long time has passed
e: My success is thanks to your support

s: The grammar pattern と言わんばかりに means 'as if to say'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'と言わんばかりに' mean?
e: She winked at me, as if to say , I love you

s: The grammar pattern 〜にたえない means 'cannot stand; cannot do; no value to'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜にたえない' mean?
a: cannot stand; cannot do; no value to
d: Without consideration for... / Despite... / Without thinking...
let alone B; not to mention; of course A but also B;
covered in st (名詞＋に＋まみれる); (名詞＋まみれ)
Because ~; ~ as expected; because ~, naturally ~; Introduces an expected reason for some circumstance that is different from the usual situation.
"thought so, but" OR, "would have thought so/imagined, but didn't"
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+にたえない. I can't stand listening to his karaoke

s: The grammar pattern ひとり〜だけでなく, ひとり〜のみならず means 'not only (A) but also (B)'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ひとり〜だけでなく, ひとり〜のみならず' mean?
d: then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: The trend in declining birth rates is not just Japan, but is similarly seen in other countries too.

s: The grammar pattern の極み means 'the utmost'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'の極み' mean?
e: height of luxury

s: The grammar pattern 〜尽くめ means 'Full of / A lot of'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜尽くめ' mean?
e: The meeting was full of good ideas.
